finding_results.notes: Reworked Lena's post-patch speech across lines 233-243. Three key changes: (1) First speech block now breaks mid-sentence with a self-interruption ('was that me, or was the system still—') followed by a head shake and restart ('I didn't know those were two things'), matching her style-bible self-correction pattern. (2) Added a concrete pool-counting example at line 237 ('This morning in the pool I lost count at forty-something. And I knew it was me who lost count. Not a correction. Not a glitch.') to anchor the realization in a specific felt behavior rather than abstract diagnosis. Physical checking gestures (pressing thumb into glass, turning hand over on the table, flexing fingers) reinforce the sensory grounding. (3) The landing at line 243 is defused: 'I don't know if better is what it is' replaces the more quotable 'I don't know if that's the same thing,' and the framing shifts from 'considered this for a long moment' to a simple physical beat. The clean thesis sentences ('Everything was just everything. The system and me were the same signal' and 'that's just how thinking works if you're doing it yourself') are removed. All chapter-critical content preserved: the quiet is easier, she confused her thoughts with symptoms, she can now distinguish, ownership does not equal improvement. Contraction level stays at Lena's moderate baseline with character-true formality in diagnostic phrasing.

summary: Single finding addressed. Lena's post-patch speech reworked from a sustained mini-essay into fragmented, self-correcting speech with a visible mid-sentence break, a concrete sensory anchor (pool stroke-counting), and a less quotable landing. The chapter's critical content and must-land beat are preserved. No other passages were modified; all p1 revisions to the Kai-Yusuf blocks-exchange and start-traces dialogue remain intact.
